question: Is the background clearly recognizable as the subway tunnel from the first input image, preserving the tile wall pattern and corridor structure?
Is the blank advertisement space on the wall free from the original stains and adhesive marks (cleanly inpainted)?
Is the manga character from the second image clearly visible and recognizable as a poster on the wall?
Does the poster retain the black-and-white screentone style of the original manga character input?
Is the poster perspective-distorted to align correctly with the vanishing point and grid lines of the tiled wall?
Does the poster exhibit visible paper texture or grain, distinguishing it from a digital overlay?
Are there visible creases, folds, or curled corners on the poster to simulate physical wear?
Are tape mounting marks visible securing the poster to the wall?
Is the neon sign text '電音夜色' from the third image clearly legible and accurate?
Is the neon sign positioned vertically above the manga poster on the wall?
Does the neon sign maintain the characteristic blue/purple glow color from the input image?
Are mounting brackets or hardware visible connecting the neon sign to the wall?
Do the mounting brackets or the sign cast small, realistic shadows onto the wall?
Is there visible colored light spill from the neon sign onto the surrounding wall tiles?
Is there visible colored light spill from the neon sign onto the floor surface?
Does the glossy floor surface show a blurred reflection of the neon sign?
Is the manga character's anatomy (e.g., hands, facial features) preserved without distortion?
Are the original directional signs and ceiling elements of the subway tunnel preserved?
Are the poster and neon sign scaled appropriately relative to the wall and each other?
Is the image free from severe blending artifacts or unnatural edges where the new elements meet the wall?
